title: Tailored Applications, Work Samples, and References · Career Claim Defense
summary: A hiring panel compares near-miss decisions for tailored applications, work samples, and references and accepts only role-relevant, truthful, accessible, privacy-preserving, and independently defensible evidence. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for auditable role-specific application packet. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Select and reorder truthful evidence from the role matrix without keyword stuffing or mirroring unsupported requirements.
Write a concise evidence bridge between the organization problem, relevant contribution, specific proof, and next conversation.
Choose or create a bounded work sample that demonstrates the prioritized behavior without exposing protected material or doing unpaid production work.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# job-search cumulative learner-controlled career campaign
# Browser labs review fictional evidence contracts only. They never contact people,
# submit applications, access accounts, expose personal data, negotiate, or accept offers.
workspace: career
candidate-id: fictional-learner
target-role: [replace with role family, work outcomes, and bounded level]
jurisdiction: [replace with location and current official guidance boundary]
audience: [replace with reviewer need, time, format, and access context]
claim: [replace with one truthful role-relevant behavior claim]
evidence: [replace with revision, artifact, observation, changed case, contribution, and limit]
process-state: [replace with discover, investigate, apply, interview, offer, close, or archive]
accessibility: [replace with keyboard, structure, reflow, status, alternatives, and human review]
privacy-consent: [replace with purpose, minimization, permission, retention, deletion, and safe contact]
decision: [replace with learner-owned next decision and stop rule]
transfer-boundary: learner-approved external actions only
review:

steps.title: Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Tailoring Map
steps.instruction:
Order the Tailoring Map evidence cycle for this quiz task before implementing it.

Evidence target: Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Tailoring Map.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Tailoring Map assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward. For Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Tailoring Map, keep Tailoring Map connected to mechanically reviewable target-role, truthful-claim, contribution, accessibility, privacy, consent, process-state, changed-candidate, failure, repair, decision, and transfer evidence.

content.text: A generic packet mechanically mirrors a listing, exposes a private work sample, and supplies reference contact details without permission. The quiz variation 2 changes the candidate background, target role, jurisdiction, access need, source date, process state, and decision constraint. Competency probe: Select and reorder truthful evidence from the role matrix without keyword stuffing or mirroring unsupported requirements.

content.title: Ordering constraint
content.text: Order work so Tailoring Map has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.

hints: For Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Tailoring Map, name the learner goal, target role, jurisdiction, audience, truthful claim, contribution, source date, evidence revision, accessibility, privacy, consent, process state, decision owner, non-claim, and transfer boundary, Tailoring Map precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Tailoring Map, trace this concrete input and predict targeting, source, evidence, contribution, access, privacy, consent, process, decision, rejection, repair, follow-up, and learner-controlled transfer evidence before reviewing it: A generic packet mechanically mirrors a listing, exposes a private work sample, and supplies reference contact details without permission. The quiz variation 2 changes the candidate background, target role, jurisdiction, access need, source date, process state, and decision constraint. Competency probe: Select and reorder truthful evidence from the role matrix without keyword stuffing or mirroring unsupported requirements.

For Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Tailoring Map, challenge the documented misconception with changed-candidate, target-role, source-date, jurisdiction, audience, access, privacy, consent, process-state, interview, offer, rejection, repair, and learner-controlled transfer evidence: Tailoring means copying every phrase from the listing.

steps.title: Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Submission Proof
steps.instruction:
Write the quiz evidence note for Submission Proof: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A generic packet mechanically mirrors a listing, exposes a private work sample, and supplies reference contact details without permission. The quiz variation 4 changes the candidate background, target role, jurisdiction, access need, source date, process state, and decision constraint. Competency probe: Verify instructions, truthful consistency, file accessibility, links, permissions, privacy, final rendering, receipt, and retained revision.

Evidence target: Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Submission Proof.

For Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Submission Proof, keep Submission Proof connected to mechanically reviewable target-role, truthful-claim, contribution, accessibility, privacy, consent, process-state, changed-candidate, failure, repair, decision, and transfer evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Verify instructions, truthful consistency, file accessibility, links, permissions, privacy, final rendering, receipt, and retained revision.
